description: Configure how users authenticate to the Verify Identity Access management interface. By default the management interface uses a local user registry, but administrators can configure a LDAP server or integrate a third party identity provider using the OIDC specification.

auth_type.description: Specifies whether the local user database or the remote LDAP user registry is used for authentication. If set to 'local', then all other fields are ignored.

ldap.description: LDAP specific configuration properties. Only one of LDAP or OIDC should be defined.

host.description: Specifies the name of the LDAP server.

port.description: Specifies the port over which to communicate with the LDAP server.

ssl.description: Specifies whether SSL is used when the system communicates with the LDAP server.

key_database.description: Specifies the name of the key database file (without any path information). This parameter is required if 'ssl' is 'true'

cert_label.description: Specifies the name of the certificate within the Key database that is used if client authentication is requested by the LDAP server.

user_attribute.description: Specifies the name of the LDAP attribute which holds the supplied authentication user name of the user.

group_member_attribute.description: Specifies the name of the LDAP attribute which is used to hold the members of a group.

base_dn.description: Specifies the base DN which is used to house all administrative users.

admin_group_dn.description: Specifies the DN of the group to which all administrative users must belong.

anon_bind.description: Specifies whether the LDAP user registry supports anonymous bind. If set to false, 'bind_dn' and 'bind_password' are required.

bind_dn.description: Specifies the DN of the user which will be used to bind to the registry. This user must have read access to the directory. This parameter is required if anon_bind is 'false'

bind_password.description: Specifies the password which is associated with the bind_dn. This parameter is required if anon_bind is 'false'.

debug.description: Specifies whether the capturing of LDAP debugging information is enabled or not.

enable_usermapping.description: Specifies whether mapping of the incoming client certificate DN is enabled.

usermapping_script.description: Specifies the javascript script that will map the incoming client certificate DN. Only valid if 'enable_usermapping' is true.

enable_ssh_pubkey_auth.description: Specifies whether or not users in the LDAP server can log in via SSH using SSH public key authentication. Defaults to false.

ssh_pubkey_auth_attribute.description: Specifies the name of the LDAP attribute which contains a user's public key data. This field is required if SSH public key authentication is enabled.

oidc.description: OIDC specific configuration properties. Only one of LDAP or OIDC should be defined.

enable_pkce.description: Specifies whether the Public key Code Exchange extension is enforced.

enable_admin_group.description: Specifies whether a user must be a member of a particular group to be considered an administrator user.

group_claim.description: The OIDC token claim to use as group membership. This claim can either be a String, or a list of Strings.

admin_group.description: The name of the group which a user must be a member of to be considered an administrator user.

user_claim.description: Specifies the OIDC token claim to use as the username.

keystore.description: The SSL Truststore to verify connections to the OIDC OP.

enable_tokenmapping.description: Specifies whether custom claim to identity mapping is performed using a JavaScript code fragment.

tokenmapping_script.description: The custom JavaScript code fragment to map an identity token to a username/group membership. Only valid if 'enable_tokenmapping' is 'true'.
